rules.title: Wire keys are camelCase, both directions
rules.trigger: an aggregate with a multi-word field (`commitSha: string`) on create/update
rules.observable: POST {"commitSha":…} persists commit_sha and reads back {"commitSha":…}; a multi-word field is never silently dropped to a 422

rules.title: Enum values use declared casing on the wire
rules.trigger: enum BuildState = Passed | Failed; a field of that type on a create body

rules.title: No persistence-internal columns leak to the wire
rules.trigger: any read of a softDeletable/auditable aggregate, or an ORM that auto-stamps timestamps
rules.observable: GET returns exactly the wireShape keys — no inserted_at/updated_at, no internal jsonb envelope

rules.title: Declared temporal fields round-trip
rules.trigger: an aggregate declaring `createdAt: instant` with an explicit create
rules.observable: POST {"createdAt":"2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"} reads back the same instant (ORM auto-value does not clobber it)

rules.title: Union-variant absence match is a presence check everywhere
rules.trigger: `find one X or absent` feeding a match { X => … | absent => … }
rules.observable: the absent arm is taken when the row is missing, identically across backends (nullable-subject ternary)

rules.title: Boolean create defaults materialize at the wire boundary
rules.trigger: `active: bool = true`; a create body omitting `active`
rules.observable: POST {} (no active) reads back {"active":true}, not a zero-value false/null

rules.title: Value-object subfields survive a jsonb round-trip
rules.trigger: valueobject Money { amount, currency } as an aggregate field on a jsonb-storing backend
rules.observable: GET returns the nested {amount,currency}; a later op reading self.price.amount does not raise

rules.title: Associations persist and preload on round-trip
rules.trigger: an op mutating a containment or X id[] ref-collection (`lines += …`, `members += t`)
rules.observable: after the op, GET nests the added child / lists the added id — no in-memory projection that omits the join write

rules.title: Error bodies converge on RFC 7807 with the 400/422 split
rules.trigger: a create violating an invariant vs a create with a malformed body
rules.observable: malformed body → 400, well-formed-but-invalid → 422; identical problem-body shape on every backend
